chapter_info.subject: Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: The Human Eye and the Colorful World

summary.category: The Human Eye and Vision Defects
points: The eye is a biological camera focusing images on the retina via a variable-focus lens system; the ability to adjust focal length is called accommodation.
Refractive defects like Myopia (corrected by concave lenses), Hypermetropia (corrected by convex lenses), and Presbyopia (aging effect) result in blurred vision due to improper focusing.
A prism disperses white light into its component colors (VIBGYOR) because different wavelengths refract through different angles; red light bends the least while violet bends the most.
Rainbows are natural spectra formed when sunlight undergoes refraction, internal reflection, and dispersion within atmospheric water droplets.
Atmospheric refraction causes stellar twinkling and shifts the apparent timing of sunrise and sunset by two minutes.
Scattering of light (Tyndall Effect) explains why the clear sky is blue (selective scattering of shorter wavelengths) and why the sun appears red at the horizon.
